message:
bd-3bxhj.1.5: Licensing & provenance guardrails for imported projects

Implements IP-risk enforcement and attribution tracking for the
OpenTUI migration platform:

- LicensingProvenanceContract with parse_and_validate pattern
- License class taxonomy (permissive, weak_copyleft, strong_copyleft,
  proprietary, unknown) with allowed/blocked disjointness enforcement
- Provenance chain validation: required stages, unbroken hash chain,
  tool version and timestamp recording
- Fail-safe defaults: missing provenance, broken chain, and blocked
  licenses all default to reject
- IP artifact assessment: worst-status propagation across dependencies
- Attribution template with required/optional field schema
- 6 risk flag definitions (no-license, copyleft-contamination,
  provenance-gap, attribution-incomplete, license-conflict, stale)

26 tests covering validation, policy enforcement, chain validation,
artifact assessment, and fail-safe behavior. Total: 136 unit tests.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.1.2","title":"task(opentui-import contracts): Build transformation policy matrix (exact, approximate, extend-ftui, unsupported)","description":"Objective: Build a decision matrix for each OpenTUI construct category: exact mapping, controlled approximation, FrankenTUI extension required, or explicit unsupported.\n\nKey outputs:\n- Policy table keyed by source construct signature.\n- Rationale and risk level per policy cell.\n- Fallback behavior and user messaging rules.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Policy matrix covers state, layout, style, effects, accessibility, and terminal capability usage.\n2. No construct lacks explicit handling class.\n3. Matrix consumed by translator planner and certification report generator.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:52.725756631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T02:06:06.823926301Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T02:06:06.823907326Z","close_reason":"Implemented versioned transformation policy matrix artifact + parser/validator/projections + unit tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","opentui-import","policy"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:52.725756631Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.2","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:36:59.073111218Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.1.3","title":"task(opentui-import contracts): Specify deterministic evidence and artifact manifest schema","description":"Objective: Define the canonical artifact manifest emitted by every migration run, including hashes, stage logs, decisions, and certification outputs.\n\nManifest fields:\n- Source fingerprint (repo commit, lockfiles, parser versions).\n- IR hash and pass-by-pass transformation hashes.\n- Generated code hash and formatter/lint versions.\n- Test outputs, benchmark outputs, and certification verdict.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Manifest is stable across repeated identical runs.\n2. Every stage writes structured JSONL records with correlation IDs.\n3. Replay tooling can reconstruct stage lineage from manifest alone.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:53.281499037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T02:21:11.803166254Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T02:21:11.803147789Z","close_reason":"Implemented: evidence manifest schema (JSON + Rust types + validation + JSONL recording + 20 tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["determinism","evidence","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:53.281499037Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.3","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:36:59.534009986Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.1.4","title":"task(opentui-import contracts): Define Bayesian confidence and risk scoring model for migration verdicts","description":"Objective: Define a calibrated Bayesian/expected-loss decision model that governs migration verdicts and adaptive controller choices across certification and rollout.

Required model components:
- Explicit state/action/loss schema (accept, hold, reject, rollback, conservative-fallback).
- Priors + likelihood updates from semantic diff, metamorphic/concolic outcomes, performance deltas, and unsupported-feature penalties.
- Calibration layer (credible intervals + conformal guardrails) with hard fallback trigger criteria.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Model inputs, priors, posterior updates, and calibration status are serialized per run and bound to claim_id/policy_id.
2. Decision output includes uncertainty bands and expected-loss rationale, not a single opaque score.
3. Auto-approve/human-review/reject/rollback boundaries are explicit, versioned, and replay-verifiable.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:53.999731818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T02:30:11.438362261Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T02:30:11.438338186Z","close_reason":"Implemented: Bayesian confidence model (JSON schema + Rust types + Beta posterior + expected-loss engine + decision boundaries + 20 tests)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["bayesian","confidence","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:53.999731818Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:36:59.991943488Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:00.444175429Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:00.896134820Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"bd-3bxhj.1.5","title":"task(opentui-import contracts): Author licensing and provenance guardrails for imported projects","description":"Objective: Enforce licensing, provenance, and IP-risk guardrails for imported projects and any advanced migration primitive used in production-facing paths.

Required outputs:
- Per-run provenance chain from source snapshot to generated artifacts (claim/evidence linkage).
- LEGAL/IP artifact records with status (clear, expired, unknown, needs-counsel) and design-around notes when relevant.
- Policy-driven fail-safe behavior for ambiguous or missing legal/provenance evidence.

Acceptance criteria:
1. Migration fails safely when required licensing/provenance data is missing, ambiguous, or policy-noncompliant.
2. Generated projects include machine-readable provenance and attribution notices.
3. Certification/release reports surface legal/IP status and unresolved risk flags with remediation actions.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:54.491457356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:20:46.949304106Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["compliance","opentui-import","provenance"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:54.491457356Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.5","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:01.351409461Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","title":"task(opentui-import contracts tests): Unit tests for contract parsers, policy evaluators, and confidence model","description":"Objective: Build comprehensive unit tests for contract schema parsing/validation, policy matrix lookup, deterministic tie-breakers, and confidence model math.\n\nRequired coverage:\n- Happy paths and malformed schema rejection.\n- Boundary thresholds for confidence-class transitions.\n- Determinism checks for same-input repeated evaluation.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Unit suite enforces >=95 percent branch coverage for contract engine modules.\n2. All failures include structured assertion logs with clause IDs.\n3. Property tests validate monotonicity/sanity constraints on confidence outputs.","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:54.952374968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-25T02:43:11.763470053Z","closed_at":"2026-02-25T02:43:11.763450256Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","opentui-import","test","unit"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:54.952374968Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:01.801955735Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:02.263004393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:02.714834271Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:03.175469354Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.1.7","title":"task(opentui-import contracts tests): E2E contract gate script with detailed structured logging","description":"Objective: Create an end-to-end script that runs contract validation against representative migration manifests and blocks CI on contract violations.\n\nScript expectations:\n- Inputs: staged artifacts + manifest + expected policy table.\n- Outputs: pass/fail verdict, per-clause report, JSONL execution trace.\n- Logging: timestamped stage spans, clause IDs, risk level transitions, remediation hints.\n\nAcceptance criteria:\n1. Script runs non-interactively in CI and local environments.\n2. Logs are replay-friendly and include deterministic run IDs and hashes.\n3. At least one intentionally failing fixture verifies red-path diagnostics.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:55.447139980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:04.897495901Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["contracts","e2e","opentui-import","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:28:55.447139980Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:03.640396018Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.7","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj.1.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:37:04.897432371Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-3bxhj.10","title":"epic(opentui-import): Alien-graveyard uplift for synthesis, formal assurance, and shadow-run adoption","description":"Background:
This epic operationalizes alien-graveyard + alien-artifact-coding methods so OpenTUI→FrankenTUI migration is not heuristic glue code, but a formally constrained, executable, replayable decision system with strong safety/fallback behavior.

North star:
Convert advanced math and systems ideas into production-grade, deterministic migration policy that improves outcomes while remaining auditable and operator-safe.

Capability pillars:
1) Contracts + provenance:
- Contracts-as-code engine, artifact-graph IDs, claim/evidence/policy/trace linkage.
2) Formal decision core:
- Closed-form Bayesian posterior + expected-loss policy + formal guarantee wrappers.
3) Graveyard executable workflow:
- Symptom-router/Fast-Start harvesting, composability/risk matrices, entry-header/graduation schema, graveyardctl verification.
4) Robust artifact-coding extensions:
- Dependence-aware conjugate fusion, sequential FDR wealth control, minimal-flip counterfactual audits, graceful degradation compiler, assumption applicability checker, L0-L3 galaxy-brain contracts.
5) Testing and proof gates:
- Deep unit/property suites + end-to-end gauntlets with deterministic replay artifacts and red-path safety diagnostics.
6) Release enforcement:
- RC promotion blocked unless all mandatory alien + optimization + certification gauntlets pass and artifact clauses are machine-valid.

Execution map (high level):
- Foundation + governance track: .10.1-.10.20
- Formal inference/guarantee track: .10.21-.10.28
- Graveyard executable track: .10.29-.10.37
- Advanced artifact-coding track: .10.38-.10.45

Success criteria:
1) Every advanced primitive/controller has budgeted mode, deterministic fallback, and replayable evidence traces.
2) Recommendation contracts are machine-linted for EV/tier, metadata schema, risk countermeasures, guarantees, comparator, rollback, and demo linkage completeness.
3) Mandatory gauntlets pass for route→rank→contract→verify→demo→release pipelines before RC promotion.
4) CI fails on missing artifacts, unsafe composition, violated assumptions, invalid guarantees, or policy-breaking optimizations.
5) Operators can reconstruct any migration decision (and nearest counterfactual flip) from structured logs alone.","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:49:38.728311183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-24T22:46:25.850012731Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alien-graveyard","formal-methods","opentui-import"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3bxhj.10","depends_on_id":"bd-3bxhj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:49:38.728311183Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

@@ -0,0 +1,92 @@
+{
+  "contract_id": "opentui-licensing-provenance-guardrails",
+  "schema_version": "licensing-provenance-v1",
+  "contract_version": "2026-02-25",
+  "licensing_policy": {
+    "allowed_license_classes": ["permissive", "weak_copyleft"],
+    "blocked_license_classes": ["strong_copyleft", "proprietary", "unknown"],
+    "copyleft_boundary_action": "fail_safe",
+    "missing_license_action": "fail_safe",
+    "ambiguous_license_action": "needs_counsel",
+    "license_class_definitions": [
+      {
+        "class_id": "permissive",
+        "description": "MIT, BSD-2, BSD-3, Apache-2.0, ISC, Zlib, Unlicense",
+        "risk_level": "low"
+      },
+      {
+        "class_id": "weak_copyleft",
+        "description": "LGPL-2.1, LGPL-3.0, MPL-2.0, EPL-2.0, CDDL-1.0",
+        "risk_level": "medium"
+      },
+      {
+        "class_id": "strong_copyleft",
+        "description": "GPL-2.0, GPL-3.0, AGPL-3.0, SSPL-1.0, EUPL-1.2",
+        "risk_level": "critical"
+      },
+      {
+        "class_id": "proprietary",
+        "description": "All-rights-reserved or custom restrictive licenses",
+        "risk_level": "critical"
+      },
+      {
+        "class_id": "unknown",
+        "description": "No license detected or unrecognized SPDX identifier",
+        "risk_level": "high"
+      }
+    ]
+  },
+  "provenance_chain_policy": {
+    "required_stages": ["source_snapshot", "extraction", "ir_normalization", "translation", "generated_output"],
+    "hash_algorithm": "sha256",
+    "chain_must_be_unbroken": true,
+    "each_stage_must_record": ["input_hash", "output_hash", "tool_version", "timestamp"],
+    "attribution_required": true
+  },
+  "ip_artifact_statuses": ["clear", "expired", "unknown", "needs_counsel", "blocked"],
+  "fail_safe_defaults": {
+    "on_missing_provenance": "reject",
+    "on_broken_chain": "reject",
+    "on_blocked_license": "reject",
+    "on_unknown_license": "hold",
+    "on_expired_attribution": "hold",
+    "on_needs_counsel": "hold"
+  },
+  "attribution_template": {
+    "format": "NOTICE",
+    "required_fields": ["original_project_name", "original_license_spdx", "original_authors", "migration_tool_version", "migration_date"],
+    "optional_fields": ["original_project_url", "modifications_summary"]
+  },
+  "risk_flags": [
+    {
+      "flag_id": "lp-no-license-detected",
+      "severity": "high",
+      "description": "Source project has no detectable license file or SPDX header"
+    },
+    {
+      "flag_id": "lp-copyleft-contamination",
+      "severity": "critical",
+      "description": "Strong copyleft dependency detected in transitive closure"
+    },
+    {
+      "flag_id": "lp-provenance-gap",
+      "severity": "high",
+      "description": "One or more pipeline stages lack provenance records"
+    },
+    {
+      "flag_id": "lp-attribution-incomplete",
+      "severity": "medium",
+      "description": "Generated NOTICE file missing required attribution fields"
+    },
+    {
+      "flag_id": "lp-license-conflict",
+      "severity": "high",
+      "description": "Incompatible licenses detected across direct dependencies"
+    },
+    {
+      "flag_id": "lp-stale-provenance",
+      "severity": "medium",
+      "description": "Provenance records older than maximum allowed age"
+    }
+  ]
+}
